title: hieracium suecicum subsp. cochleatum
intro: Hieracium suecicum subsp. cochleatum, also known as the cockle-leaved hawkweed, is a rare and endangered plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat wounds and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Hieracium suecicum subsp. cochleatum include its potential to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, although its use should be under the guidance of a medical professional.
